diagrams.title: Basic Polygon Mesh Structure
diagrams.description: Shows a simple 3D cube representing fundamental mesh components
elements: 8 vertices (corners)
12 edges (connecting lines)
6 faces (square surfaces)
Labels showing V=8, E=12, F=6
diagrams.purpose: Understanding fundamental mesh elements and their relationships

diagrams.title: Winged-Edge Data Structure
diagrams.description: Diagram of one edge showing pointers to adjacent elements
elements: Central edge with two endpoint vertices
Two faces on either side of edge
Four adjacent edges (winged in both directions)
Arrows showing bidirectional pointers
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how winged-edge stores mesh connectivity for efficient traversal

diagrams.title: Half-Edge Structure
diagrams.description: Shows one edge split into two directed half-edges
elements: Edge AB split into half-edges A→B and B→A
Each half-edge stores: start vertex, opposite half-edge, face, next half-edge
Clockwise/counterclockwise traversal around faces
Color coding for each half-edge's data storage
diagrams.purpose: Understanding half-edge data structure for face and edge operations

diagrams.title: Mesh Subdivision Process
diagrams.description: Shows one iteration of subdivision on a quad
elements: Original quad with 4 corner vertices
New edge points at midpoint of each edge
New face point at center of quad
Resulting 4 quads from one original quad
Smooth curve approximation after multiple iterations
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how subdivision increases mesh resolution and smoothness

diagrams.title: Manifold vs Non-Manifold Edges
diagrams.description: Comparison of manifold and non-manifold edge configurations
elements: Manifold edge: shared by exactly 2 faces
Non-manifold edge: shared by 3+ faces
Boundary edge: shared by only 1 face
Dangling edge: connected to only one vertex
Visual examples of each type
diagrams.purpose: Understanding mesh topology constraints for valid mesh processing
